task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the Juris-M application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Open the Advanced Search dialog to configure the search parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Advanced Search' or 'Search' popup window is open within Juris-M, displaying rows for adding specific filter conditions.

task_completion.description: Set a search condition to filter for the United States Supreme Court.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the search dialog, there is a condition row with a field like 'Court', 'Institution', or 'Publication' set to an operator like 'contains' or 'is', and a value containing 'Supreme', 'SCOTUS', 'U.S.', or 'US'.

task_completion.description: Set a search condition to filter for cases decided after 1960.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the search dialog, there is a condition row with a field like 'Date' or 'Year' set to an operator like 'is after' or '>', and the value '1960'.

task_completion.description: Save the search with the name 'Post-1960 SCOTUS Decisions'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left sidebar of the Juris-M main window (under 'My Library'), there is a Saved Search item (typically indicated by a folder icon with a magnifying glass) explicitly named 'Post-1960 SCOTUS Decisions'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Juris-M graphical user interface rather than using terminal commands or scripts to modify the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Juris-M GUI being actively used (e.g., dropdowns opened, text typed into input fields) rather than a terminal window executing SQLite commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually created a saved search rather than just renaming a standard folder or typing the expected name into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Post-1960 SCOTUS Decisions' item in the sidebar has the distinct Saved Search icon (folder with a magnifying glass), not a standard collection folder icon, and no text editors are used to fake the output.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually delete items from the library to simulate a filtered view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main library still appears to contain various items (like law review articles or older cases) when the 'My Library' root is selected, proving the filtering is done via the Saved Search and not by deleting records.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a Saved Search in Juris-M named exactly 'Post-1960 SCOTUS Decisions'. The search must include two mandatory conditions: 1) A date condition filtering for cases after 1960 (e.g., Date/Year > 1960). 2) A court condition filtering for the Supreme Court (e.g., Court contains 'Supreme', 'SCOTUS', or 'U.S.'). The final verification relies on seeing this properly named Saved Search in the left sidebar of the Juris-M application.
